title: Finding future value using compound interest
problem: A principal of $\$3500$ is invested at $4\%$ per annum compound interest for $3$ years. Find the future value.

steps.instruction: Write the compound interest formula and identify the values.
Substitute the values into the formula.
Multiply to find the future value and round to the nearest cent.

title: Finding present value given a future value
problem: What principal must be invested at $5\%$ per annum compound interest for $4$ years to produce a future value of $\$6000$? Round to the nearest cent.

steps.instruction: Start with the compound interest formula and rearrange to make $PV$ the subject.
Divide to find the present value.

title: Finding the number of years by trying consecutive values
problem: An investment of $\$4000$ earns compound interest at $6\%$ per annum. After how many complete years will the investment first be worth more than $\$5000$?

steps.instruction: Write the inequality to solve, then try consecutive integer values of $n$.

steps.working: The investment first exceeds $\$5000$ after $4$ complete years.
